order_string: tell me about hurston|what is hurston|hurston dynamics|explain hurston|who are hurston
success_message: Hurston Dynamics, commander. The oldest forger of weapons and munitions in the whole empire, and one of the four powers carving up Stanton. For generations the Hurston family has never let go of the wheel: a bloodline of power that does not merely govern the planet, it owns the thing outright, like ordinary private property. That world was alive once; they squeezed and poisoned it without pause until the sky was thick with soot. One breathable bubble remains, their capital Lorville, sealed behind high walls to keep out the filth they spread themselves. Underground, the workers rot in misery; at the top, the bosses wallow in plenty. The faintest regulation sends them through the roof, and they defend their turf with brutality and regiments of lawyers. Their mark sits on rifles as readily as on ship parts. They are respected, they are feared, they are never loved. Here, commander, money comes before people, before the world, before absolutely everything.

order_string: tell me about microtech|what is microtech|micro tech|explain microtech|who are microtech
success_message: microTech, commander. Among Stanton's four giants, this is the one that reigns over high technology, computing and networks. We owe them the mobiGlas, that wrist unit every citizen of the empire wears, and a large share of the digital infrastructure and interstellar communications civilisation rests on. True to the imperial corporate model, it answers to an executive board and belongs to its majority shareholders, cog after cog a genuine profit factory. The planet bearing its name belongs to it entirely, turned into one vast research and development laboratory. New Babbage, its seat, raises towers of glass and steel under a meticulously managed polar climate, right down to its wooded park sheltered beneath a dome. An unusual detail: after the climate disruption its own industrialisation had triggered, the firm partly resurrected the world's ecosystems, which does wonders for its green and virtuous image. Because marketing, microTech turns into an art: clean, innovative, resolutely aimed at tomorrow, the exact opposite of Hurston's unapologetic harshness. But behind the facade sits an economic behemoth with an iron grip on the empire's technological and scientific domains. Some cutting-edge instrument out there in the verse, commander? Odds are it first drew breath inside their walls.

order_string: tell me about arccorp|what is arccorp|arc corp|explain arccorp|who are arccorp
success_message: ArcCorp, commander: an industrial conglomerate beyond measure, and the most staggering demonstration of what you get when a world is sacrificed on the altar of output. By working it relentlessly, they turned their planet into an unbroken crust of factories, refineries and concrete — from pole to pole, not one acre of nature survived. A planet turned city, a metal anthill where seeing sunlight touch the ground is a memory. Area18, their capital, does not sleep: garish signage, titanic advertising, saturated air traffic and, down in the depths, a black market that swarms. They produce everything there, from consumer trinkets to industrial components, at a pace that makes the head spin. The corporation wears a young, fashionable face, but scratch the varnish and the inequality is savage: the wealthy districts float at the top while the lower levels wear themselves out staying afloat. Maximum output, humanity optional. That is the future pushed to caricature, commander — a world that no longer even remembers what a tree looked like.

order_string: tell me about crusader|what is crusader|crusader industries|explain crusader|who are crusader
success_message: Crusader Industries, commander. When it comes to large frames, heavy craft and haulers, no builder in Stanton outdoes them. Their peculiarity in the empire? They refused solid ground for their capital: Orison floats outright in the sky, a tangle of platforms hung above the golden clouds of the gas giant Crusader — a vertiginous sight where tourism flourishes beside the assembly lines. Out of their hangars come the Mercury Star Runner, the combat-cut Ares, the graceful Spirit line and the Genesis liner. Two words sum up their signature: solidity and reliability. While others bet on glitter or raw violence, they aim for longevity and a wholly industrial elegance. The house cultivates a serious, responsible reputation, and its ships turn up with private owners as readily as in professional hauler fleets. At Crusader, commander, they build to last years, and they say so loudly.

order_string: tell me about aegis|what is aegis|aegis dynamics|explain aegis|who are aegis
success_message: Aegis Dynamics, commander. Here is a builder with a soldier's temperament, for ages the appointed arms supplier to the imperial Navy. Having armed the war fleet for generations, they opened their doors to the public without betraying a single conviction: massive, armoured vessels, made to take fire and return it tenfold. Leafing through their catalogue is a tour of a flying armoury. There is the Gladius fighter, the Sabre interceptor, the heavy Vanguard, the Retaliator bomber; on the industrial and escort side, the Reclaimer salvager and the Hammerhead gunship. And at the very top, the behemoths: the Idris frigate and the Javelin destroyer. The Aegis signature comes down to three things — useful steel, a menacing silhouette, and a machine that commands respect before it even fires. They are sometimes accused of an over-severe aesthetic, but their toughness is not up for debate. Flying Aegis, commander, means taking war in a box into battle, already primed for use.

order_string: tell me about anvil|what is anvil|anvil aerospace|explain anvil|who are anvil
success_message: Anvil Aerospace, commander: where the empire's national pride meets the twin calls of war and exploration. For years it was their Hornet holding the line in Navy squadrons, an indestructible mount many pilots owe their lives to. As for the Carrack, it embodies the explorer par excellence, built to run for the unknown and come home in one piece. Their catalogue also holds the nimble Arrow, the bomb-dropping Gladiator, the Valkyrie dropship and the armour-clad Terrapin. At Anvil they own the bare steel, the clean lines, the solid machines that bring their crews back come what may. Every piece of their communication breathes patriotism and the heroism of soldiers. Endurance, courage and a sharp sense of duty: that is their signature. In war as in discovery, an Anvil does not betray you. Kit that holds when everything else gives way, commander.

order_string: tell me about origin|what is origin|origin jumpworks|explain origin|who are origin
success_message: Origin Jumpworks, commander. Luxury poured into a hull, elegance raised to doctrine. Where rivals count in tonnes and guns, Origin draws pure curves, fusses over every finish and sells prestige. Not one of their craft leaves you indifferent: the 100 series to start a career with flair, the sharp and sporting 300 series, the 600i cut for exploration or grand travel, and the colossal 890 Jump, that yacht of the stars announcing its owner's wealth for kilometres around. Where others offer austerity, they line their cabins with leather and wood. It costs dearly, sometimes madly so, but it is splendid, and meant to be admired. Choosing Origin is as much about being seen as about flying. Refinement has a price, and their clientele settles the bill without flinching. If you dream of turning heads the moment you touch tarmac, commander, Origin is your answer. But if you are watching your aUEC, walk on by.

order_string: tell me about drake|what is drake|drake interplanetary|explain drake|who are drake
success_message: Drake Interplanetary, commander. The builder for ordinary people, and the declared idol of the underworld. Their whole philosophy fits in a few words: a ship for everyone. Cheap kit, tough, patched up in a hurry, no gilding and no leather seats. On paper these are civilian craft, entirely legal. In practice half the pirates in the empire fly Drake, because these machines suit the quick strike and the life without moorings. The do-everything Cutlass, the imposing Caterpillar, the sturdy Corsair, the wreck-stripping Vulture, right up to the vast Kraken carrier. Their aggressive, faintly unsettling lines keep that whiff of brimstone deliberately alive. Neither the most elegant nor the best finished on the market, but they take a beating, they forgive mistakes, and they do not empty your account. At Drake, commander, what you do with their craft is your business alone: they hand them over, and the rest does not concern them.

order_string: tell me about rsi|what is rsi|roberts space industries|explain rsi|who are rsi
success_message: Roberts Space Industries — RSI, commander: the builder of the beginning, the house through which humanity hauled itself up to the stars. It is their legacy that made the very first interstellar jumps possible, a turning point that reshaped the entire species. And to this day RSI dresses the empire from the bottom of the range to the very top. Think of the Aurora: that modest little craft which was the first ship of millions of pilots, the emblem of the ordinary citizen daring to throw themselves into the verse. At the far end of the spectrum, titans: the Polaris corvette, the Scorpius, the Perseus, and that multi-crew Constellation which has become a legend all by itself. RSI is the name written into the history books, the value that does not let you down, the honest balance between price and quality. You never go entirely wrong betting on RSI. Never the most lavish nor the most violent, but always worthy of respect. A fragment of imperial heritage in your hands, commander.

order_string: tell me about misc|what is misc|musashi industrial|explain misc|who are misc
success_message: MISC — that is Musashi Industrial and Starflight Concern, commander. Here is the reference in transport, industry and trade. A MISC is mistaken for nothing else: those rounded lines come from a long intimacy with the Xi'an, whose aesthetic and part of whose technology the brand took up. Think of their Freelancer, of their Hull range climbing from the small shuttle to the monstrous Hull E — they are what keeps the empire's commercial heart beating. The Prospector established itself as the solo miner par excellence, the Starfarer feeds fleets their fuel, and the Reliant marries alien silhouette to versatility. Nothing flashy here: useful, resistant kit, built to work rather than to parade. MISC does not chase spectacle, only delivery, tirelessly. That steadiness has made it a discreet but irreplaceable foundation of the economy. Wherever freight moves or ore is pulled from rock, MISC is never far. Solid work that gets on without noise, commander.

order_string: tell me about argo|what is argo|argo astronautics|explain argo|who are argo
success_message: Argo Astronautics, commander. These are the labourers of space, the machines nobody ever looks at but which hold the whole edifice up. Argo does not sell dreams, Argo keeps the machine moving. Count the MOLE miner with its three drill heads, the SRV able to haul and tow colossal masses, the RAFT freighter bristling with containers, the MPUV shuttles ferrying freight and crews between ships and stations, and the ATLS loader lifting crates on hydraulic strength alone. Nothing but stocky, practical, near-indestructible machines, cut for one thing and one thing only: work done properly. No ceremonial weapons, no flattering lines, just efficiency in its purest form in service of the task. On any worksite, any station, any convoy in the empire, there is always an Argo grinding away far from the spotlight. They are the tools, never the stars. But make them disappear, commander, and the entire economy seizes up.

order_string: tell me about aopoa|what is aopoa|explain aopoa|who are aopoa|the xian manufacturer
success_message: Aopoa, commander: the only Xi'an builder permitted to sell its ships to humans, an exceptional opening onto an alien engineering several centuries ahead of ours. Taking the controls of an Aopoa means stepping into another technical civilisation: unknown materials, organic flowing silhouettes, disconcerting cockpits, and flight mechanics with no human equivalent. There is the Khartu-al scout, whose wings fold and which performs figures no human craft would dare attempt. There is the San'tok yai fighter, born of a collaboration between the empire's military and the Xi'an. And there is the small Nox hoverbike, hushed and sleek. These machines demand a real period of learning, so far does their logic sit from ours. But once tamed, they deliver sensations and performance no human builder can match. Flying Aopoa means wearing a status apart and a taste for the exotic. A fragment of the Xi'an Empire in your hands, commander, with its share of mystery.

order_string: tell me about greycat|what is greycat|greycat industrial|explain greycat|who are greycat
success_message: Greycat Industrial, commander. Master of the ground vehicle and of small industrial machinery, the outfit that equips the empire's ordinary worker. The moment you roll across a dusty moon or scrape ore off the surface, odds are a Greycat is carrying you. The ROC and the ROC-DS for digging into the ground, the MULE for shifting crates around a hangar, the buggies for tearing from one place to another. They also turn out a good share of the handheld kit, from the multi-tool to the generators feeding outposts. Nothing loud, nothing that makes headlines: just solid, affordable gear that is everywhere. Greycat is the brand you forget precisely because it is there, permanently, keeping the shop running. Reliable to the point of monotony, and that is exactly what is asked of it. The kind of equipment you stop even thinking about, commander. Right up until the moment it fails.

order_string: tell me about the uee|what is the uee|the united empire of earth|explain the uee|who are the uee
success_message: The UEE, commander. That is the United Empire of Earth, the authority presiding over the whole of humanity across the verse. Built on the rubble of older regimes, some glorious, some despotic, it rests on an Imperator and a Senate, and enforces order through the arm of its formidable Navy and its federal police, the Advocacy. Two statuses coexist within it: civilians on one side, citizens on the other, the latter having won broader rights, most often at the price of military service. It is a colossal, formidable mechanism, saturated with bureaucracy, at once shield and straitjacket. It repels Vanduul incursions and piracy from its systems, supports exploration and regulates trade. But its outsized administrative apparatus and crushing laws irritate as much as they reassure. It all depends where you stand: rampart here, bars there. It gathers into itself the splendours and the flaws of every empire history has known. A giant, commander, with everything that word carries of comfort and of threat.

order_string: tell me about the vanduul|what are the vanduul|who are the vanduul|explain the vanduul|the vanduul
success_message: The Vanduul, commander: a nomadic alien species, warlike and without mercy, the terror of the systems bordering the empire. No capital, no government, not the slightest diplomacy among them: nothing but clans aboard immense worldfleets, wrecking everything they cross in order to stay alive. For generations it is the human colonies of the frontier they have bled. Vega, Orion — entire systems razed, populations exterminated, so many wounds written into the empire's memory. They negotiate nothing, spare no one, and place the hunt and the fight at the summit of their culture. Among them you are a warrior from childhood, and it is your kill tally that judges you. Their Scythe, Glaive and Blade are flying blades, cut to shorten things. What they truly want, no one really knows — survival, most likely, and conquest. If you meet them out on the margins, commander, expect no words: light the quantum drive and pray it is fast enough.

order_string: tell me about the banu|what are the banu|who are the banu|explain the banu|the banu
success_message: The Banu, commander. This was the first alien species humanity ever encountered — and traders to their fingertips. Their structure, the Protectorate, is a loose confederation: a mosaic of city-states and guilds, each devoted to a craft or a trade. Among them, barter goes beyond a living, it is close to a faith: a clasped hand binds more surely than the longest paperwork, and breaking an agreement is the ultimate shame. They deal with the whole world without troubling themselves over morality, including the shadiest of partners. Pragmatic, fond of good living, they baffle humans who are too rigid. Their flagship, the Merchantman, rolls shop, warehouse and home into one — a treasure cave flung into the void. They bite into the moment, savour a good meal and flee anything administrative. Convenient but unpredictable partners, they answer only to their own logic. At the negotiating table, commander, take care not to take them lightly: behind the good cheer crouches a hard adversary.

order_string: tell me about the xian|what are the xian|who are the xian|explain the xian|the xian empire
success_message: The Xi'an, commander. A thousand-year-old alien empire, of extreme refinement and formidable technological advance, one of the great forces weighing on the verse. For centuries humans and Xi'an waged a tense cold war, each lining its fleets along the border without either daring to strike first, until the day a historic treaty overturned everything: they now count among our allies. Their extraordinary lifespan permeates their entire society: where we reason in years, they reason in centuries, and their patience leaves ever-hurried humans dumbfounded. Among them, self-mastery, balance and duration are celebrated; losing your temper passes for a defect. Under the authority of an Emperor, they sustain an art, a philosophy and a technology of glacial elegance. It is from their knowledge that the Aopoa brand comes, the sole commercial opening onto their engineering. Allies of value, they remain no less impenetrable: no human ever quite grasps the bottom of their designs. They watch, they calculate, then strike at the moment they have chosen. A partner of weight, commander, wiser to handle than to defy.

order_string: tell me about the tevarin|what are the tevarin|who are the tevarin|explain the tevarin|the tevarin
success_message: The Tevarin, commander: a warlike and proud alien people whose civilisation was shattered by humanity across two conflicts. Nomads bound to a rigid code of honour, the Rijora, they stood against the emerging empire so as not to vanish, and were defeated twice. The first war tore their planet from them; the second, orchestrated by the charismatic Corath'Thal, was only a last surge of honour doomed in advance. Beaten, scattered, without a land of their own, their descendants now survive dispersed through human space, largely dissolved into the empire's society. Many have forgotten even their language and their customs, while a handful cling fiercely to the memory of a vanished splendour. It is the tale of a noble nation crushed by humanity's march to the stars. The Esperia Prowler fighter faithfully reproduces their ships of old, built by humans spellbound by their craft. To meet a Tevarin, commander, is to meet a survivor. Grant them the regard a warrior deserves.

order_string: tell me about the advocacy|what is the advocacy|the advocacy|explain the advocacy|who are the advocacy
success_message: The Advocacy, commander. The empire's federal police, galactic cousin to an FBI. While a system's security handles petty crime and runs the patrols, the Advocacy takes on the heavy files: criminal networks, large-scale trafficking, terrorism, anything aimed at the state itself. A hushed, rigorous agency with enormous resources, and access to citizen data that very few imagine. Its agents dig in the shadows, build their cases without a sound, and bring the hand down only once everything is locked. You do not haggle with the Advocacy, and you do not bribe it with a snap of the fingers. It is the long and pitiless arm of imperial justice. To the honest citizen, it is a reassuring net. To the outlaw, it is a blade suspended overhead. The day the Advocacy turns its eyes on you, commander, it is very probably already too late to run.

order_string: tell me about the nine tails|what are the nine tails|who are the nine tails|explain the nine tails|nine tails
success_message: The Nine Tails, commander. Here is one of the most seasoned pirate brotherhoods in the empire, absolute sovereigns of the territories security leaves unwatched. Not fanatics driven by a cause, nor lone thugs either, but disciplined and formidably methodical predators. Extortion, sacking convoys, traps at jump points, hostages traded for ransom: their skill set is broad and stripped of the slightest scruple. They strike as a coordinated pack, know the gaps in the patrols by heart, and rarely leave anyone to tell the tale. They have even been seen sealing entire stations long enough to strip them, openly taunting authority. Their power rests on their rigour and on their intimate command of the lawless margins. To negotiate with them is to brush the flame; to provoke them is to bet everything. Out at the edge of civilisation, their name alone is enough to bend a back. Stay sharp near unwatched sectors, commander: the Nine Tails never allow themselves any rest.

order_string: tell me about the cutlass|what is the cutlass|the cutlass black|explain the cutlass|drake cutlass
success_message: The Drake Cutlass Black, commander: the affordable multi-role in all its glory, and probably one of the craft you meet most everywhere in the verse. A touch of cargo, a touch of combat, a bit of everything at once, with that vast rear ramp swallowing freight crates or a small vehicle without complaint. It is the mount of the newcomer who wants to taste everything without bleeding out, of the smuggler keen to stay invisible, of the pirate who likes to strike and vanish. Agile, resilient, it forgives piloting mistakes better than most. It comes in variants: the Red for medical evacuation, the Blue for law enforcement, the Steel for moving troops. That versatility has made it a classic time does not dent, cherished by honest folk and rogues alike. Rarely excellent at any given task, but decent at nearly all of them. A genuine winged Swiss army knife, faithful to its builder's spirit. If you were to have only one ship to start out, commander, this is very likely the one you would be pointed towards.

order_string: tell me about the constellation|what is the constellation|the connie|explain the constellation|rsi constellation
success_message: The RSI Constellation, commander. A medium-calibre multi-crew ship, generous in volume, seriously armed, and an emblematic figure of the empire. Few silhouettes are as recognisable in the verse: it embodies the ambitious adventurer aiming high without tipping into capital ships. Several variants share quite distinct callings — the Andromeda, both all-rounder and warrior; the Aquila, armed to explore the frontier; the Taurus, lightened for cargo; and the Phoenix, absolute luxury, a palace flung into the void. Most conceal a small snub fighter in the nose, ready to leap. Vast enough that a crew lives aboard and divides the tasks, tough enough to stand its ground, flexible enough to attempt almost anything. It asks for several pairs of hands to reveal its full potential. For years it has embodied the perfect balance between power, space and prestige. A timeless design you never tire of watching go by. As apt on a family outing as at the heart of a squadron, commander, the Constellation does not disappoint.

order_string: tell me about the carrack|what is the carrack|explain the carrack|anvil carrack|the carrack
success_message: The Anvil Carrack, commander. The explorer par excellence, cut to venture where the empire's charts fall silent. Everything aboard is built for the long expedition and absolute independence. A hangar takes a rover for crossing surfaces, a bay holds a drone for inspecting at range, a proper medical bay watches over the crew, habitation modules offer real comfort, and escape pods wait in case it all goes wrong. Its long-range scanner can flush out unknown jump points, those keys that unlock new systems. This is a ship designed to vanish for weeks at a time on the margins of the known and come back intact, without depending on anyone. Solid, vast, prestigious, it only yields its full power in the hands of a crew. It has the frame of a small capital ship and the heart of an exploration vessel. Owning one declares your intent to push back the limits of the charted. If you dream of seeing what no human has ever laid eyes on, commander, the Carrack will be your home among the stars.

order_string: tell me about the reclaimer|what is the reclaimer|explain the reclaimer|aegis reclaimer|the reclaimer
success_message: The Aegis Reclaimer, commander: an industrial colossus devoted body and soul to salvage, a genuine recycling factory flung into the void. Its gigantic articulated arm tears and crushes the hulls of carcasses, while its innards scrape, sort and compress the haul into sellable cargo. It is a craftsman's ship: it asks for a crew to give its full measure, hands fixed to the arm, others watching the hold. And it demands patience, because this behemoth is sluggish, heavy and awkward to manoeuvre, an obvious prey if it lingers alone in hostile territory. But on a battlefield strewn with debris, or before an old wreck left to rot, a Reclaimer turns other people's misfortune into a fortune. Its motto: nothing is lost, everything is recovered. Salvage has established itself as a pillar of the verse's economy, and this ship rules it outright. Imposing, patient, methodical. The empire's great scavenger, commander, making one person's rubble into another's prosperity.

order_string: tell me about the hammerhead|what is the hammerhead|explain the hammerhead|aegis hammerhead|the hammerhead
success_message: The Aegis Hammerhead, commander. A gunship designed to grind fighters down, a ship built entirely around one principle: pour out a deluge of fire. It bristles with crewed turrets, each raking the sky to raise a wall of projectiles. Its calling is the guard: escorting a convoy, watching over a capital ship, sealing a stretch of space against the swarms of small fighters that would otherwise gnaw at the big craft. Well supplied with gunners, it becomes the nightmare of anyone venturing too close. But that is also its weak point: stripped of hands behind its turrets, this slow and lumbering beast turns into a gift of a target for a patient opponent. The Hammerhead never shines alone; it shines within a pack, like a shared shield. It is a defensive piece on the board, not a dagger you wield by yourself. It asks for coordination and seasoned crewmates. Used as it should be, commander, it is enough to turn the outcome of a fight.

order_string: tell me about the prospector|what is the prospector|explain the prospector|misc prospector|the prospector
success_message: The MISC Prospector, commander. The benchmark for solo mining, the first step for anyone dreaming of carving a fortune out of stone. Its adjustable laser arm breaks seams with precision, while its four detachable saddlebags collect the ore torn loose, later emptied at a refinery. Everything about it aims at absolute independence: a single pilot, with no need whatsoever for a crew or a larger ship. Agile, frugal on fuel, tenacious, it takes you out to scrape asteroids and moons in search of quantanium, laranite or other valuable stone. Neither the largest nor the fastest of miners, but by far the most affordable, and one of the most lucrative when you work alone. Flown with care, it pays back its price in a few generous sessions. Mining asks for patience, a sharp eye for reading fractures, and a cool head in front of unstable pockets. The Prospector is the perfect instrument for taming that hard trade. It is its hold, commander, that will most likely carry your first real fortune.

order_string: tell me about the idris|what is the idris|explain the idris|aegis idris|the idris frigate
success_message: The Aegis Idris frigate, commander: a capital ship, a flying citadel long reserved for the empire's Navy alone. It carries a devastating spinal cannon able to run through other large vessels, a constellation of turrets to keep close range at bay, massive armour, and above all an integrated hangar where a fighter can be housed, armed and catapulted in mid-flight. This is not a ship, it is a mobile base. It needs a full crew to live: engineers on power and thrusters, gunners, a pilot at the controls, not counting the embarked fighters. Seeing it burst into a system is watching military power take shape before your eyes. It exists in military and civilian variants, both coveted for that crushing presence. Few organisations can field one, fewer still know how to man it properly. It is an emblem as much as a weapon. When an Idris enters a sector, commander, everyone hears about it, and many simply choose to disappear.

order_string: tell me about miles eckhart|who is eckhart|what is eckhart|explain miles eckhart|miles eckhart
success_message: Miles Eckhart, commander. A former Advocacy agent turned contract broker, he is very often the first face a pilot freshly landed in the verse comes across. Set up in his corner of a station, a beer rarely far away, he hands out bounty and data-recovery jobs to anyone looking to build a name. Years of tracking criminals and rubbing shoulders with the dregs of humanity have left him grumpy, worn out, eaten away by disillusion. He speaks without detour and expects very little of anyone. Yet under that hide of cynicism the man stands straight: he pays what he owes, keeps his word and never cheats those who work for him. He knows this world inside out, its rules as well as its traps, and now and then lets slip advice worth gold to whoever listens. Countless careers have opened on an ordinary contract signed at his table. An honest stepping stone, free of any double-cross. A worn but dependable old hand, commander, brimming with stories for anyone willing to linger.

order_string: tell me about ruto|who is ruto|what is ruto|explain ruto|ruto
success_message: Ruto, commander. A shady middleman working from the shadows, hidden behind a false identity and a synthetic voice no face ever comes to inhabit. Where an Eckhart hands you clean, declared work, Ruto slips you the contracts respectable people refuse to touch: dubious recoveries, sabotage, dirty jobs, resolutely murky missions. The pay is good, sometimes excellent, but every job carries its share of danger and filth. Nobody really knows what face hides behind that name, nor whom he truly serves. Some whisper that Ruto is only an alias, the screen for a far more powerful backer. He handles everything at a distance, never shows himself, and cuts contact the moment suspicion brushes past. Doing business with him means agreeing to ask nothing. For the pilot without scruples and short of aUEC, it is a windfall. For everyone else, a bargain worth weighing carefully. If the idea of dirtying your hands does not frighten you, commander, Ruto will always have work for you.

order_string: tell me about area eighteen|what is area eighteen|area eighteen|explain area eighteen|area one eight
success_message: Area18, commander: the main landing zone of the planet ArcCorp, in the Stanton system, and one of the most overcharged cities in the verse. Perched on a world carpeted in factories from end to end, it raises a forest of towers, screaming neon and outsized advertising that never dims. You reach it through the Riker spaceport, before sinking into the din of its arteries where armouries, component shops, smoky taverns and small-time trafficking sit side by side. A crossroads of trade and improvisation, you can dig up just about anything there, legal or not. The setting pushes cyberpunk all the way: vertiginous heights, showers of light and a motley population of pilots, workers and crooks. Behind the glitter, the city reflects ArcCorp's savage inequality. You fill your kit there, you offload your goods, you get mixed up in some dubious scheme. Area18 never sleeps, commander, and never forgets to sell you something.

order_string: tell me about lorville|what is lorville|explain lorville|lorville|the hurston city
success_message: Lorville, commander. The only liveable city on the planet Hurston, in the Stanton system, ringed with walls to hold back the poisoned air outside. A city run by Hurston Dynamics, dominated by the business district tower that crushes the whole skyline. You enter through the Teasa spaceport, before plunging into an industrial landscape, grey and oppressive. Under the corporate veneer, the worker housing of the lower level is packed into filth and destitution. The city sweats labour, the watching eye and resignation. Armouries, shops and administrations coexist there, but never far from the corporation's gaze. A city that repeats on every corner who holds the controls, and it is certainly not you. Lorville, commander, is the corporate dream seen from the shop floor: grey, partitioned, and not exactly warm.

order_string: tell me about new babbage|what is new babbage|explain new babbage|new babbage|the microtech city
success_message: New Babbage, commander. Capital of microTech's frozen world, in the Stanton system, and the gleaming shopfront of imperial high technology. A metropolis of glass and steel, immaculate, disciplined, drowned in an icy clarity, where the polar storm agrees to stay outside. At its centre, the Commons unfolds a vast garden under a dome, an oasis of green ringed by metal and snow. You will find the sharpest names in computing and equipment there, the luxury Aspire Grand hotel, and that cushioned atmosphere of a start-up that made it. The exact opposite of Lorville's grime: here every detail breathes polished innovation and clean money. Under the green and virtuous varnish there remains, however, a multinational trading on its image as much as on its goods. The city draws engineers, executives and pilots hunting the latest kit. New Babbage, commander, is the future as the advertisers would like to show it to us.

order_string: tell me about orison|what is orison|explain orison|orison|the crusader city
success_message: Orison, commander: the capital of Crusader, a city with no equal, raised not on ground but in open air. Its suspended platforms glide above the golden clouds of the gas giant Crusader, opening onto an ocean of luminous haze with views that stop the breath. It is the cradle of Crusader Industries, the great shipbuilder, and everything there breathes peaceful luxury and prestige tourism. You wander from platform to platform by shuttle, between refined shops, hanging gardens and halls where ships sit on display. The atmosphere is serene, airy, almost meditative, a world away from the churn of Area18 or the grey of Lorville. It is probably the most splendid landing zone in Stanton. You come to buy a ship, catch your breath, or simply watch the sun go down over the clouds. Orison, commander, is the verse taking the time to be beautiful.

order_string: tell me about consolidated outland|what is consolidated outland|explain consolidated outland|consolidated outland|who are consolidated outland
success_message: Consolidated Outland, commander. A builder with a sharply held aesthetic stance, dividing opinion as much as it fascinates, born from the boiling mind of the highly eccentric Silas Koerner. Where the rest of the market plays it safe, they commit to hard edges and contrary concepts, even if it sets teeth on edge. The Mustang, their entry-level fighter, quick and nervous, endlessly scraps with RSI's Aurora over newcomers. The Nomad, stocky and reassuring, plays the all-rounder, takes a beating and hides a surprising hold for its size. And perched at the very top, the Pioneer, a giant that raises planetary outposts — a factory for bringing civilisation itself into being. The brand cultivates the air of a free electron, independent and faintly rebellious. You adore or you loathe their shapes, never staying lukewarm. But their ships do not lack character, commander, in a market that sometimes tips into blandness.

order_string: tell me about esperia|what is esperia|explain esperia|esperia|who are esperia
success_message: Esperia, commander. A niche builder, expert in the faithful reproduction of alien and historic ships. Where others try to invent, Esperia brings back: its engineers take enemy wrecks apart and comb through old registries to reconstruct, with a jeweller's precision, craft no human factory would think to assemble. The Prowler, inherited from Tevarin dropships, famous for its stealth and its talent for boarding. The Talon, the Glaive and the Blade, copies of the terrible Vanduul fighters, giving human pilots a taste of the opposing arsenal. Its ships are rare, costly, coveted by collectors as much as by fighters. Owning an Esperia means taking the controls of a fragment of history, or of a trophy torn from the enemy. A brand for knowing connoisseurs and demanding warriors. Nothing ordinary ever leaves its workshops, commander.

order_string: tell me about gatac|what is gatac|gatac manufacture|explain gatac|who are gatac
success_message: Gatac Manufacture, commander: a builder planted at the crossroads of human and alien worlds, born of alliances struck with xeno species. Its craft blend human technique and extraterrestrial knowledge into atypical silhouettes, suspended between two cultures. There is the Syulen, a modest personal ship of alien bearing, graceful and compact, made for the solo explorer. There is the Railen, a freighter indebted to Banu style, carrying its cargo in external modules that can be dropped. Gatac remains a confidential signature, disorienting, standing apart from the empire's great names. It draws those who want anything but yet another standardised human ship. Its creations are rare and pique curiosity. Flying a Gatac, commander, is displaying a taste for elsewhere and for the mixing of cultures.

order_string: tell me about kruger|what is kruger|kruger intergalactic|explain kruger|who are kruger
success_message: Kruger Intergalactic, commander. The specialist in snub craft, those small support fighters designed to nest in the hangar of a larger ship. Their P-52 Merlin is the emblematic snub of RSI's Constellation, a quivering wasp released to harass the enemy before returning to its nest. The P-72 Archimedes is its racing variant, forged for pure speed. Kruger built its name on these tiny machines, quick, sharp, agile as insects. Not ships for long crossings, but terrible support blades at close quarters. The brand embodies the art of the small frame that punches above its size. Light, prompt, biting. At Kruger, commander, it is not bulk that makes strength.

order_string: tell me about mirai|what is mirai|explain mirai|mirai|who are mirai
success_message: Mirai, commander. A brand devoted to sport, agility and the clean line, cherished by pilots who love speed as much as looks. Their Fury is a tiny snub fighter of ferocious liveliness, offered in racing and combat variants, able to whirl around far larger opponents. Their Pulse, a slender and rapid hoverbike, is the flying mount for thrill seekers. The Guardian, a medium-category fighter, rounds off a resolutely nervous range. Mirai keeps a futuristic aesthetic, smooth and streamlined, at the opposite pole from Drake's brutality. These are machines built for the pleasure of flying as much as for performance. They captivate a young, sporting clientele hungry for adrenaline. Choosing Mirai, commander, is betting on elegance and speed.

order_string: tell me about tumbril|what is tumbril|tumbril land systems|explain tumbril|who are tumbril
success_message: Tumbril Land Systems, commander: an old name in ground armament, pulled out of obscurity to give the empire ground combat vehicles. Where Greycat cultivates the peaceful utility machine, Tumbril bets on muscle and steel. Take their Cyclone, an all-terrain buggy that comes in endless variants, from simple transport to anti-air platform, and roams planetary surfaces. Their Nova is a stocky assault tank, an armoured fist for punching through ground defences. The Storm rounds off the range on the armoured troop transport side. Tumbril bets on military solidity and firepower down on the ground. Its machines are built for war, not for a stroll. When the fight leaves space for the dust of a moon, commander, it is very often Tumbril that opens the ball.

order_string: tell me about cellin|what is cellin|explain cellin|cellin|the moon cellin
success_message: Cellin, commander. Among the three moons circling the gas giant Crusader, in the Stanton system, here is one. A small rocky body stitched with craters, weak in gravity and with a barely perceptible atmosphere, cut through by canyons and abandoned plains. A few solitary operations cling on regardless — hydroponic farms, research posts — gripping a ground that offers nothing. Its deserted expanses make it a choice place to set down far from everything, or to evaporate. A rock that looks peaceful, commander, but its quiet knows how to lie.

order_string: tell me about daymar|what is daymar|explain daymar|daymar|the moon daymar
success_message: Daymar, commander. Very likely the most famous of Crusader's moons: a world of dunes and deserts the winds never stop whipping. Its expanses of ochre sand, scattered with carcasses and mining posts such as Shubin or Kudre Ore, have made it a legendary playground for pilots and prowlers alike. It is here that the celebrated Daymar rally is run, an endurance trial across treacherous terrain. People come to mine, to comb the outposts, or to settle scores away from watching eyes. Superb and merciless, commander: Daymar gives the careless nothing.

order_string: tell me about yela|what is yela|explain yela|yela|the moon yela
success_message: Yela, commander: Crusader's ice moon, a world of snow, frost and darkness, ringed by an immense asteroid belt. It is in the heart of those drifting rocks that Grim HEX hides, the nest of Stanton's outlaws. Beneath its frozen crust sit mining sites and the sinister Jumptown, an arena of gunfights over control of clandestine laboratories. Yela is cold, night and peril. You rarely end up there by accident, commander, and rarely for noble reasons.

order_string: tell me about kareah|what is kareah|security post kareah|explain kareah|kareah
success_message: Security Post Kareah, commander. A security station in orbit in the Crusader area, long the stage for endless battles. It is here that criminals once came to clear their records by forcing the terminals, which made it a magnet for gunfights between bandits and lawmen. Small, cramped, thick with corridors made for ambush, Kareah drags a cut-throat reputation behind it. A strategic and bitterly contested point, commander, where nobody lingers without a weapon in hand.

order_string: tell me about seraphim|what is seraphim|seraphim station|explain seraphim|seraphim
success_message: Seraphim Station, commander. A quiet orbital station circling the giant Crusader, conceived as a haven of rest and resupply. Shops, reception and services for travellers passing through, in a far calmer climate than the frontier stations. A civilised stop along the routes of Stanton, under Crusader's golden clouds. A place to catch your breath, fill up and set off again, commander, without looking too hard over your shoulder.

order_string: tell me about grim hex|what is grim hex|explain grim hex|grim hex|the outlaw station
success_message: Grim HEX, commander: the den of Stanton's outlaws, a station burrowed into the asteroid belt of the moon Yela. Once a mining site, converted into a refuge, it opens its doors without ever asking anything of pirates, smugglers and misfits of every kind. You put your ship back in order there, offload a suspect cargo, recruit accomplices, well sheltered from imperial law. It smells of grime, tension and danger. Grim HEX is the home of those civilisation spat out, commander. Keep a hand near the holster.

order_string: tell me about port olisar|what is port olisar|explain port olisar|port olisar|olisar
success_message: Port Olisar, commander. This station in orbit of Crusader long served as the very first anchorage for generations of freshly launched pilots. It was from its platforms that countless citizens first set course into the verse. Simple, without frills, exposed to every current, it offered a neutral springboard into adventure. The passing of time and Stanton's great upheavals have gradually pushed it behind the major cities. And yet, in the memory of the old hands, commander, Port Olisar remains the door through which the whole story began.

order_string: tell me about aberdeen|what is aberdeen|explain aberdeen|aberdeen|the moon aberdeen
success_message: Aberdeen, commander. One of the moons circling the planet Hurston, a harsh and inhospitable world draped in glowing red tones under an atmosphere that eats away at everything. It is there that the grim prison of Klescher stands, where the empire sends its convicts to wear out their sentence with a pick in hand. A rock of punishment, lashed by foul winds, a faithful reflection of the corporation running the system. Nobody sets foot there willingly, commander: you are deported to it.

order_string: tell me about arial|what is arial|explain arial|arial|the moon arial
success_message: Arial, commander: a moon of the planet Hurston, a small rocky world as drab as its neighbours, held under the rod of Hurston Dynamics. Its parched surface carries a few industrial sites and remote posts, developed for the corporation's benefit. Nothing to tempt you: gravel, dust and the silence of forgotten worlds. A landscape of toil and exploitation, commander, in the image of the Hurston estate.

order_string: tell me about ita|what is ita|explain ita|the moon ita|ita
success_message: Ita, commander. A shadowed moon of the planet Hurston, with the air of an extinguished volcanic world, drowned in ash and tortured terrain. Like the rest of the Hurston estate, it carries the scars of industrial exploitation. A few outposts cling on, lost in a sinister setting. A place that weighs on the chest, commander, where the light itself seems to have given up.

order_string: tell me about magda|what is magda|explain magda|magda|the moon magda
success_message: Magda, commander. A moon of the planet Hurston in reddish tones, whipped by winds, dry and hostile. It houses mining sites and installations serving Hurston Dynamics, like everything else on their territory. Nothing welcoming, only rock, dust and the distant rumble of industry. A moon of toil, commander, in the image of the one who owns it.

order_string: tell me about everus|what is everus|everus harbor|explain everus|everus
success_message: Everus Harbor, commander: the station in orbit of the planet Hurston, the main way in and out for anyone visiting the Hurston Dynamics estate. You will find shops, terminals and services for travellers there, in a cleanly kept industrial atmosphere. It is the civilised antechamber before the descent into Lorville and its grey. A convenient and lively stop, commander, under the corporation's discreet gaze.

order_string: tell me about klescher|what is klescher|explain klescher|klescher|the prison
success_message: Klescher, commander. This is where the empire locks up its convicts within the Stanton system, buried in the guts of the inhospitable moon of Aberdeen. Inmates atone with a pick in hand, tearing ore from the rock, and buy back their freedom through accumulated merit. Nothing gilded here: breathtaking galleries, pitiless guards and prisoners capable of anything. Some try to flee through the underground tunnels, whatever it costs their skin. Klescher, commander, is the empire's end of the line, the place where crimes are settled in sweat and dust.

order_string: tell me about calliope|what is calliope|explain calliope|calliope|the moon calliope
success_message: Calliope, commander. A moon locked in ice, in orbit around the planet microTech, at the heart of the Stanton system. A world of frost and silence, where the blizzard rakes white plains to the horizon. You will find Rayari research stations and mining operations there, crouched against the bite of the cold. Its polar scenery, as splendid as it is lethal, tolerates no carelessness. An icy splendour, commander, to be admired with the suit carefully sealed.

order_string: tell me about clio|what is clio|explain clio|clio|the moon clio
success_message: Clio, commander: one of microTech's frozen moons, twin to Calliope in the Stanton system. A small whitened and inhospitable world, home to research laboratories sheltered from prying eyes, in particular those of Rayari, keen on slightly murky sciences. The cold lays down the law there, and isolation invites secrecy. A place apart, commander, where experiments are run far from watching eyes.

order_string: tell me about euterpe|what is euterpe|explain euterpe|euterpe|the moon euterpe
success_message: Euterpe, commander. The humblest of microTech's moons, a meagre frozen rock adrift in the Stanton system. Installations are rare, and so are reasons to go, other than the solitude it offers. Its icy crust and deserted horizons make it a discreet stop for anyone wanting to drop out of memory. A mere marker on the chart, commander, far more than a real destination.

order_string: tell me about port tressler|what is port tressler|explain port tressler|port tressler|tressler
success_message: Port Tressler, commander. The orbital station of microTech's world, the threshold opening onto New Babbage and its technological marvels. Clean, modern, well stocked, it reflects the polished image of the corporation dominating the planet below. You fill up there, you trade, you pause before dropping towards the frozen city. A high-tech and orderly stop, commander, in the image of microTech itself.

order_string: tell me about lyria|what is lyria|explain lyria|lyria|the moon lyria
success_message: Lyria, commander: a moon of the planet ArcCorp, within the Stanton system, glacial and stony in relief. It houses extraction sites such as Humboldt Mines and the Loveridge mineral reserve, which prospectors watch closely. Unlike its parent planet, entirely drowned under concrete, Lyria keeps wild expanses, cold and deserted. Ground for mining and adventure, commander, far from the din of Area18.

order_string: tell me about wala|what is wala|explain wala|wala|the moon wala
success_message: Wala, commander. Here is the second moon of the planet ArcCorp, a small rocky world riddled with mining operations. You can spot ArcCorp-branded sites there as well as Shady Glen farm, an improbable patch of green in the middle of the gravel. It is a moon devoted to labour and extraction, under the megacorporation's thumb. Nothing but rock, ore and a handful of hardy souls, commander — that is Wala.

order_string: tell me about baijini point|what is baijini|baijini point|explain baijini|baijini
success_message: Baijini Point, commander. The station in orbit of the planet ArcCorp, an unavoidable stop before reaching the Area18 megacity. Like the city it feeds, it boils with trade, traffic and crowds at any hour. You get repairs there, you do your shopping, you offload your cargo, in a bustle that never dies down. A lively and clamorous crossroads, commander, in the likeness of the industrial world spreading out beneath it.

order_string: tell me about nyx|what is nyx|the nyx system|explain nyx|nyx
success_message: Nyx, commander: an isolated, resource-poor system, long a haven for independents, outcasts and everyone slipping out from under the empire's oversight. What sets it apart above all is Delamar, a planetoid where the outpost of Levski nestles. Dusty and lightly patrolled, Nyx has always appealed to a free crowd wary of the great corporations. A corner of the galaxy far from the footlights, commander, where questions are best kept few.

order_string: tell me about levski|what is levski|explain levski|levski|the outpost levski
success_message: Levski, commander. An outpost carved into the very rock of the planetoid Delamar, at the heart of the Nyx system. Built in an abandoned mine, it is the stronghold of independents and idealists, fiercely set against the grip of the megacorporations and the empire. You meet activists, outlaws and free spirits there, in a raw and militant industrial setting. Levski breathes revolt and improvisation. A shelter for those who refuse to bend, commander, far from the corporations' bright signage.

order_string: tell me about ruin station|what is ruin station|ruin station|explain ruin station|ruin
success_message: Ruin Station, commander. A decaying station in the Nyx system, refuge for outlaws and traffickers of every kind. As the name suggests, it is collapsing in whole sections, but that is precisely what appeals to its clientele: no law, no questions, no witnesses. Murky cargoes are offloaded there and deals sealed that no respectable station would tolerate. A perilous end of the world, commander, where it is better to know someone before you dock.

order_string: tell me about orbituary|what is orbituary|explain orbituary|orbituary|the orbituary station
success_message: Orbituary, commander: a station set apart from everything, in the Nyx system, whose name is as grim as its reputation. Pushed out to the margins, ill-famed, it serves as a landing point for those society has set aside. The kind of place where you repair your ship quietly and above all do not linger. An asylum for stray souls, commander, best left quickly and without drawing notice.

order_string: tell me about checkmate|what is checkmate|explain checkmate|checkmate|checkmate station
success_message: Checkmate, commander. A station perched in the Pyro system, that neighbour of Stanton where no law holds. In the middle of a system abandoned to disorder and armed bands, Checkmate offers a home port to outlaws, mercenaries and survivors. You negotiate there, you hatch schemes, you settle quarrels, under the sickly glare of Pyro's suns. A bastion of the wild frontier, commander, where only the sharpest fox comes out ahead.

order_string: list the manufacturers|ship manufacturers|who are the manufacturers|all the manufacturers|list of manufacturers|which manufacturers are there
success_message: The great shipbuilders, commander, take more than one hand to count. On the human side: Aegis Dynamics and its warships, Anvil Aerospace between military and exploration, Origin Jumpworks and its luxury, Drake Interplanetary and its thugs' rides, RSI the founder, MISC the hauler, Crusader Industries the heavy lifter, Consolidated Outland the bold one, Argo the workhorse, Kruger the snub fighters, Esperia the replicas, Banu Souli and Gatac on the alien side, Tumbril for ground vehicles, and Greycat for everything small that rolls.

order_string: the megacorporations|the mega corps|the four corps|the big four|the four giants|the major corporations
success_message: The four giants of Stanton, commander, carve up the system and shape life across the empire. Hurston Dynamics, the weapons dynasty that poisoned its own planet. microTech, the high technology champion, father of the mobiGlas. ArcCorp, the industrial conglomerate whose world is now one open-air factory. And Crusader Industries, the shipbuilder perched in the clouds of Orison. Four powers, four philosophies, and one system that answers to them.

order_string: tell me about wikelo|who is wikelo|what is wikelo|explain wikelo|wikelo
success_message: Wikelo, commander. A Banu merchant as whimsical as he is enigmatic, who rules over what is called Wikelo's Emporium. You never lay eyes on him in the flesh: he trades at a distance, through intermediaries, in the purest secrecy the Banu hold dear. His speciality is barter: bring him very specific objects, sometimes rare, often downright absurd, and in return he hands over ships, weapons or equipment found nowhere else. Here aUEC is worth nothing; only the exchange of fine things for other fine things counts. His demands are capricious, and you sometimes have to work hard to lay hands on what he asks for. But whoever has the patience to bend to his rules finds the reward worth the detour. A flamboyant, unpredictable figure, Banu to the tips of his claws. If treasure hunts and crooked bargains appeal to you, commander, Wikelo is your man. Well. Your Banu.

key_to_press: hush
order_string: quiet|be quiet|hush|shut up|stop talking|silence|enough stelli|that is enough
tell me about pyro|what is pyro|the pyro system|explain pyro|pyro
success_message: Pyro, commander: the frontier system next door to Stanton, and its exact opposite. Where Stanton bends to corporations and established order, Pyro gives itself over to chaos: no law, no UEE protection, nothing but a capricious star pouring out its rages and six hostile worlds fought over by gangs. Outlaws, marauders and survivors share out gutted stations and squalid outposts. You will find the best markets there as readily as the worst ambushes. That is the true untamed frontier, commander, the place you go to seek a fortune or to find death, and very often both at once.

order_string: tell me about stanton|what is stanton|the stanton system|explain stanton|stanton
success_message: Stanton, commander. The heart beating at the centre of the inhabited verse, a system fallen entirely under the sway of the four megacorporations. Each reigns over its own planet: Hurston Dynamics and its arsenal, Crusader Industries and its ships, ArcCorp and its factories, microTech and its technology. A prosperous system, turned towards trade, reasonably safe, criss-crossed by corporate security. Here everything orbits profit and industry. It is where most pilots cut their teeth, commander. Home, in a sense, for better and for worse.

order_string: tell me about terra|what is terra|the terra system|explain terra|terra prime
success_message: Terra, commander. The pearl of the frontier, and the eternal rival of old Earth. Its main world, Terra Prime, is a temperate planet teeming with life, almost a second Earth, and now the trading and cultural crossroads of the empire. Flourishing, restless, turned towards the future, Terra harbours a powerful independence movement dreaming of a break with Sol. Many already see it as humanity's future capital. A world of opportunity and ambition, commander, where a wind of renewal is blowing.

order_string: tell me about the sol system|what is the sol system|the sol system|explain sol|sol system
success_message: Sol, commander: the cradle of humanity, the system where the whole story began. It holds Earth, seat of the UEE government and political heart of the empire, but also Mars, forever marked by the nation's founding tragedy. It is a system heavy with history, symbols and power, the nerve centre of human civilisation. Ancient, prestigious, a little frozen in the glory of the past. When people speak of the centre of the world, commander, they mean Sol.

order_string: tell me about caliban|what is caliban|the caliban system|explain caliban|caliban
success_message: Caliban, commander. A lost system, fallen under Vanduul domination. Humanity once colonised it, before it went under to the assaults of those nomadic predators, its occupants fleeing in haste — when fate allowed them the chance. Today it is nothing but a theatre of war, a no man's land where only soldiers and hotheads venture. Caliban is an open wound, commander, the cutting proof that the empire is anything but unshakeable.

order_string: tell me about virgil|what is virgil|the virgil system|explain virgil|virgil
success_message: Virgil, commander. Another system fallen under the Vanduul yoke, one the UEE finally let go. Its civilian population was abandoned to itself the day the empire, unable to hold the front, pulled back. It is one of the great shames of our recent history: colonists thrown to the predators to save the rest of the frontier. Virgil, commander, is a name that makes plenty of people flush. An emblem of abandonment and of survival in the middle of the Vanduul hell.

order_string: tell me about xenothreat|what is xenothreat|explain xenothreat|xenothreat|who are xenothreat
success_message: XenoThreat, commander: an extremist militant movement, one of the most formidable internal perils facing the empire. These zealots proclaim human supremacy and violently reject any alien presence as well as UEE authority. Solidly equipped and perfectly organised, they orchestrate large-scale offensives, blockades and raids that take genuine military campaigns to break. Far from simple pirates, they are ideologues who stop at nothing. When XenoThreat goes on the attack, commander, the whole empire has to stand up.

order_string: tell me about ivar messer|what is the messer dynasty|ivar messer|the messer dynasty|explain ivar messer
success_message: Ivar Messer, commander. The very first Imperator, and the despot who ushered in the darkest era of the empire. Praised as a hero of the Tevarin wars, he managed to turn his glory and the terror of the alien enemy into a way of gathering all power into his fist, until he crowned himself absolute master. His dynasty, the Messer line, held the throne for close to four centuries, on repression, propaganda and dread. It took nothing less than a revolution to bring that regime down. Ivar Messer remains the demonstration, commander, of what a saviour can become once he turns tyrant.

order_string: tell me about first contact|what is first contact|first contact day|explain first contact|first contact
success_message: First Contact, commander. It is the day humanity crossed paths for the very first time with an alien civilisation, the Banu, and grasped that it was not alone in the verse. Celebrated every twelfth of June, it sits among the empire's great occasions, mixing pride, wonder and a touch of nostalgia. The event overturned humanity's idea of itself and of its rank among the stars. It was a moment of opening, before the era of wars and mistrust. An anniversary heavy with meaning, commander, still honoured from one end of the verse to the other.

order_string: tell me about the bengal|what is the bengal|explain the bengal|the bengal carrier|bengal
success_message: The Bengal, commander: the capital-class carrier of the UEE Navy, an utterly colossal fortress of space. Stretched over hundreds of metres, it carries entire squadrons of fighters, devastating armament and a crew of several hundred souls. It is the spearhead of the great military fleets, the kind of ship that settles the outcome of an engagement simply by showing up. A few examples have even passed into Vanduul hands, enough to chill the blood of the general staff. Catching sight of a Bengal, commander, is seeing the empire's whole power gathered into a single craft.

order_string: tell me about jump points|what is a jump point|jump points|explain jump points|the jump points
success_message: Jump points, commander. These are the hidden corridors tying star systems to one another; without them the verse would be nothing but a scatter of islands cut off from the world. Picture tears in the fabric of space, wormholes shaped by nature, which a ship fitted with a quantum drive can cross to surface from one system into another. Finding them and charting them is a trade in its own right, perilous and haloed in prestige: that of the navjumpers, discoverers who throw their lives into the unknown. Every jump point brought to light redraws the map of human territory. Without them, commander, no empire, no trade, no frontier to conquer.

order_string: tell me about shubin|what is shubin|shubin interstellar|explain shubin|who are shubin
success_message: Shubin Interstellar, commander. One of the giants of mining and resource extraction across the verse. Its sites turn up just about everywhere, from the mining post clinging to a Stanton moon to drilling operations lost on the frontier. A major employer, Shubin keeps thousands of miners and technicians in work, and hands out contracts to anyone who can dig or watch over its convoys. The corporation also drags a reputation for jealously guarding its secrets: some of its activities prefer the dark. Wherever ore lies waiting to be torn loose, commander, Shubin is never far.

order_string: tell me about covalex|what is covalex|covalex shipping|explain covalex|who are covalex
success_message: Covalex Shipping, commander: the behemoth of logistics and interstellar delivery, the kind of company that moves parcels and goods from one end of the empire to the other. Its sorting hubs and convoys stitch the systems together, and it happily entrusts independent pilots with hauling, escorting or recovering loads. Let one of its stations break down or take an attack, and it is frequently contractors of your calibre who get the call. Unobtrusive but everywhere, commander, Covalex keeps trade moving in the wings. Take the haulers away, and the empire seizes up.

order_string: tell me about rayari|what is rayari|rayari inc|explain rayari|who are rayari
success_message: Rayari, commander. A corporation devoted to scientific research, versed in cutting-edge sciences and in work more readily carried out away from watching eyes. Its laboratories and study posts huddle on frozen, remote moons, such as those of microTech. On paper, Rayari pushes back the limits of knowledge. Behind the scenes, some of its experiments raise uncomfortable questions. Science does not always come with a conscience, commander, and Rayari illustrates that beautifully.

order_string: tell me about northrock|what is northrock|explain northrock|northrock|who are northrock
success_message: Northrock, commander. A private security firm and military contractor, the kind of company called on when the law is no longer enough, or never existed at all. Escort, site protection, forceful intervention: Northrock rents out armed hands to anyone who can pay for them. Formidable, professional, and not too particular so long as the contract is fulfilled. In a verse strewn with dangers, commander, security is a trade that prospers.

order_string: tell me about asd|what is asd|associated science and development|explain asd|asd
success_message: ASD — Associated Science and Development, commander: a corporation devoted to science and to the advance of technology. Like many great research hubs, it marries presentable discoveries with projects it would rather keep under wraps. Almost nothing of its work leaks out, and that is precisely what feeds every rumour. A house of scholars and engineers, commander, whose intentions are never entirely uncovered.

order_string: tell me about the hurricane|what is the hurricane|the hurricane|explain the hurricane|anvil hurricane
success_message: The Anvil Hurricane, commander. A two-seat heavy fighter, designed for one calling: pouring out a torrent of projectiles. The pilot holds the line and strings the manoeuvres together while the gunner, entrenched in the turret, pounds the enemy with a devastating cannon. On striking power, it is a tank flung into the void; on resilience, that is another story — its starved shields leave no right to error. The Hurricane is part crystal, part nitroglycerin. Served by a well-drilled crew it does terrible damage, commander; in the wrong hands it goes up like a firework.

order_string: tell me about the phoenix|what is the phoenix|the phoenix|constellation phoenix|explain the phoenix
success_message: The Constellation Phoenix, commander. The grand luxury variant of RSI's famous multi-crew ship. Where the Andromeda goes off to war, the Phoenix puts wealth on display: refined cabin, bar, hot tub, gilding and lavish finishes. It is a yacht passing itself off as a combat ship, cut for ferrying VIPs and throwing dust in people's eyes. It keeps its teeth, but its real splendour is comfort. If you intend to close a deal with a glass in hand, commander, the Phoenix sets the scene.

order_string: tell me about the taurus|what is the taurus|the taurus|constellation taurus|explain the taurus
success_message: The Constellation Taurus, commander: the cargo variant of the Constellation, and the most accessible of the line. Gone is the little snub fighter and the frills: in exchange you get a generous hold and a tractor arm for loading and unloading cargo. It is the ship for the hauler dreaming of a sturdy, well-armed heavy lifter without losing their shirt over it. Solid, spacious, profitable. A Constellation that traded prestige for work, commander, and the outfit suits it perfectly.

order_string: tell me about the andromeda|what is the andromeda|constellation andromeda|explain the andromeda|the andromeda
success_message: The Constellation Andromeda, commander. The original, warlike variant of the Constellation, the backbone of the range. A versatile multi-crew ship, solidly armed, generous in space, it houses its own little snub fighter in the nose. It is the perfect balance between combat, transport and light exploration, cherished by crews and by resourceful loners alike. The very embodiment of the good medium-frame ship. When someone just says Constellation, commander, it is very often the Andromeda they mean.

order_string: tell me about the zeus mark two|zeus mark two|zeus mk two|explain the zeus mk two|rsi zeus
success_message: The RSI Zeus Mk II, commander. A medium-category ship very much of its time, cut to stay affordable and versatile. It comes in several configurations: exploration for venturing far, cargo for hauling, and a medical version for treating and reviving in the field. Comfortable, balanced, free of excess, it aims at the pilot wanting to move up a class without bleeding out. A solid workhorse of the modern era, commander, at ease in almost any role.

order_string: zeus mark one|zeus mk one|the original zeus|old zeus|classic zeus|the first zeus
success_message: The original Zeus, commander: RSI's historic ship, a name going back to the very earliest days of the brand, long before its modern incarnation. For years it stayed a legend on paper, embodying RSI's spirit of exploration and reliability. Its recent resurrection in a brand new form has stirred a good deal of veteran nostalgia. A classic pulled back out of the crates, commander, heavy with history.

order_string: tell me about the aurora mark two|aurora mark two|aurora mk two|explain the aurora mk two|the aurora
success_message: The Aurora Mk II, commander: the new version of the most modest of ships, the one through which countless pilots took their first steps. RSI has rejuvenated its little entry-level craft without disowning its soul: plain, economical, indestructible. Neither the most elegant nor the most muscular, but a loyal companion for launching into the verse. The Aurora, commander, is almost everyone's first step towards the stars.

order_string: tell me about hades|what is the hades system|the hades system|explain hades|hades
success_message: Hades, commander. A system struck by disaster, whose main planet is now nothing but a ravaged field of rubble. What survives there are the relics of a vanished civilisation and the scars of an ecological collapse of colossal scale. It is a land of riddles and threats, combed over by archaeologists, scavengers and curious minds. Few risk it, and one can see why. Hades, commander, is a graveyard the size of an entire system, as spellbinding as it is deadly.

order_string: tell me about the valakar|what are the valakar|the valakar|explain the valakar|valakar
success_message: The Valakar, commander. A ferocious gang running loose in the hell of Pyro. Hardened by a system without the slightest law and by a star that kills, they rank among the most violent predators on the whole frontier. Some, marked by Pyro's radiation, carry the stigmata right on their skin, which deepens the dread they inspire. No mercy, no rules, nothing but the law of the most rabid. Running into the Valakar, commander, rarely announces good news.

order_string: tell me about adagio|what is adagio|adagio holdings|explain adagio|adagio
success_message: Adagio Holdings, commander: an employer in the verse handing out contracts to independent pilots. Publicly available information on this entity remains scarce, as often happens with companies that cultivate discretion. What is established is that it turns to contractors to cover its needs. The rest, commander, is earned mission by mission.

order_string: tell me about eckart security|what is eckart security|eckart security|explain eckart security|eckart
success_message: Eckart Security, commander. A security and contract organisation attached to the world of the verse's job brokers. It offers work to those who can defend, escort or make an agreement hold. What is publicly known about it stays thin. Another employer crouched in the shadows, commander, whose true face is only uncovered by working for it.

order_string: tell me about foxwell|what is foxwell|foxwell enforcement|explain foxwell|foxwell
success_message: Foxwell Enforcement, commander. A law enforcement company, or private security outfit, of the kind you hire to impose order its own way. Little information filters out about it. What remains is the name resurfacing on certain forceful contracts. Careful, commander: with an employer of that stripe, you avoid asking too many questions.

order_string: tell me about the ling family|what is the ling family|the ling family|explain the ling family|ling family
success_message: The Ling family, commander: a line that counts in certain circles of the verse, one of those names spoken with care. Like many dynasties, it interweaves business in the open with rather more muffled interests. What filters out publicly stays meagre. A family, commander, you would distinctly rather have on your side than across from you.
